records.ety: [Dim. of crumb, v. t., akin to D. kruimelen G. krümeln.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crumbled (krm"b'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Crumbling (krm"blng).]

defs: To break into small pieces; to cause to fall in pieces.
quotes: He with his bare wand can unthread thy joints, And crumble all thy sinews.

defs: To fall into small pieces; to break or part into small fragments; hence, to fall to decay or ruin; to become disintegrated; to perish.
quotes: If the stone is brittle, it will crumble and pass into the form of gravel.
The league deprived of its principal supports must soon crumble to pieces.
